acceptance_criteria: Grader JSON exhibits no internal contradictions: passed/failed flags consistent across all fields.
metadata_json.weights are explicit and used to compute final_score; computed contribution fields are labeled and match final_score arithmetic.
pathway_evidence_table.xlsx contains a plain-text 'Summary' worksheet and an 'Assumptions' worksheet, or a companion JSON/CSV reproducing normalized actual_outputs is attached.
Verifier can parse at least one artifact preview (either .xlsx via openpyxl in a suitable runtime or the companion JSON) and confirm grounding.
Updated attempt id 145-revised is present in trace with updated grader/verifier refs.

commands_to_execute: 1) Open the artifact and add 'Summary' and 'Assumptions' worksheets (plain text).
2) Export normalized actual_outputs to pathway_evidence_table.json and attach to attempt artifacts.

3) Update grader output generation script to set metadata_json.weights and metadata_json.computed_weighted_score_breakdown with clear labels and to propagate pass/fail decisions from a single decision routine.
4) Re-run grading and verification and attach resulting grader/verifier IDs to the attempt.

commands_to_execute: 1) Open the artifact and add 'Summary' and 'Assumptions' worksheets (plain text).
2) Export normalized actual_outputs to pathway_evidence_table.json and attach to attempt artifacts.

3) Update grader output generation script to set metadata_json.weights and metadata_json.computed_weighted_score_breakdown with clear labels and to propagate pass/fail decisions from a single decision routine.

review_checks: Manual review of grader JSON for pass/fail consistency
Automated check that sum(weights) == 1.0 and final_score == sum(weights * rubric_scores)
Verify presence of 'Summary' and 'Assumptions' worksheets in the workbook or presence of companion parsed artifact file
Re-run verifier and confirm no 'internal_consistency_pass_fail_labels' or 'inconsistent_weight_labeling' issues remain

planned_changes.description: Unify grading outputs: update grader JSON to ensure top-level passed boolean, failed_criteria array, passed_criteria array, and every rubric_item_scores[].passed are all consistent and derived from the same internal calculation.

Standardize weight reporting: keep metadata_json.weights as the single source of truth (weights sum to 1.0) and add metadata_json.computed_weighted_score_breakdown with clearly labeled 'contribution' values. Add a new metadata_json.final_score_derivation string that shows the arithmetic calculation.

Embed a plain-text 'Summary' worksheet into pathway_evidence_table.xlsx that lists: assumptions (percent formatting, tie handling), needs_review samples (if any), recommended next steps, and instructions for domain experts. Also add an 'Assumptions' worksheet enumerating all conventions.

Materialize a machine-readable companion file (pathway_evidence_table.json or .csv) that reproduces the normalized actual_outputs (samples array, validation summary). Attach this file to the attempt artifacts to allow preview without openpyxl.

Re-run deterministic precheck and model grader/verifier after making the changes. Capture and attach a successful artifact preview (or the companion JSON) and update trace entries to reference the authoritative grader_result_id and verifier_result_id.
Update the trace/log note documenting any runtime dependency issues encountered (e.g., openpyxl unavailable) and the mitigation used (companion JSON or re-run in environment with openpyxl).

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Fix grader/verifier JSON inconsistencies and improve artifact traceability so verifiers can evaluate deliverable even when .xlsx parsing is unavailable.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The baseline attempt produced the required artifact and correct per-sample QC logic, but the grader/verifier outputs contain internal inconsistencies (conflicting pass/fail flags for ri_4 and inconsistent weight labeling). Additionally, the environment could not parse the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable), leaving uncertainty about presence of a plain-text 'Summary' or 'Assumptions' worksheet required for expert follow-up. These issues reduce trust in the declared overall pass status despite an arithmetic final_score that matches the intended weighting.
